segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: Good morning.
segments.zh: 早上好。我们被要求选择欧洲服装或时尚的一个方面，并谈论其随时间的发展。我决定专注于一个相当小的服装领域，那就是口袋。我选择口袋有两个原因。我们都有口袋——比如在牛仔裤、夹克、大衣里——即使我们经常也带包或公文包，但没有什么比能把手机或信用卡塞进口袋更方便的了。然而，我猜想，除此之外，人们并不会过多考虑口袋，它们作为时尚单品被忽视了。

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: Now, we've been asked to choose an aspect of European clothing or fashion and to talk about its development over time.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: I decided to focus on a rather small area of clothing and that's pockets.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: I chose pockets for two reasons, really.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: We all have them – in jeans, jackets, coats, for example – and even though we often carry bags or briefcases as well, nothing is quite as convenient as being able to pop your phone or credit card into your pocket.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: Yet, I suspect that, other than that, people don't really think about pockets too much and they're rather overlooked as a fashion item.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: It's certainly very interesting to go back in time and see how pockets developed for men and women.
segments.zh: 回顾过去，看看口袋如何为男性和女性发展，确实非常有趣。在18世纪，时尚与现在大不相同，口袋也是如此。我们先考虑男性时尚……那是西装流行的时代。裤子只到膝盖，被称为“马裤”，马甲很短，外套很长，但这三件衣服都衬有布料，口袋由顾客选择的裁缝缝进这些布料中。穿着者可以随身携带小物件，如铅笔或硬币，并通过衬里的缝隙取用。对于那些想要看起来时尚的男性来说，外套口袋在外观上变得越来越装饰性，但如果穿着者是专业人士，比如医生或内科医生，需要携带医疗器械，口袋通常更大但更朴素。

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: In the 18th century, fashions were quite different from the way they are now, and pockets were too.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: If we think about male fashion first … that was the time when suits became popular.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: Trousers were knee-length only and referred to as 'breeches', the waistcoats were short and the jackets were long, but all three garments were lined with material and pockets were sewn into this cloth by whichever tailor the customer used.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: The wearer could then carry small objects such as pencils or coins on their person and reach them through a gap in the lining.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: Coat pockets became increasingly decorative on the outside for men who wanted to look stylish, but they were often larger but plainer if the wearer was someone with a profession who needed to carry medical instruments – a doctor or physician, for example.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: The development of women's pockets was a little different.
segments.zh: 女性口袋的发展则有些不同。首先，它们远不如男性口袋那么显眼或容易触及。在18和19世纪，女性随身携带许多物品，其中一些可能价值不菲。女性更容易遭遇盗窃，尤其是富有的女性，她们总是担心扒手。所以，她们的做法是制作一对用绳子系在一起的口袋。口袋由布料制成，如果穿着者钱不多，可能用回收布料，或者用更昂贵的材料，如亚麻布，有时还带有非常精致的刺绣。女性将口袋系在腰间，使其垂在衣服下面。记住，那时裙子很长，在通常穿着的多层衬裙之间有足够的空间隐藏各种小物品。她们会在裙子的褶皱处开一个口子，通过它取用所需物品，比如香水。当然，劳动女性也需要随身携带她们工作或行业可能用到的物品，但她们的口袋仍然在衣服内侧，只是变得更大或更长——有时甚至垂到膝盖！

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: For one thing, they weren't nearly as visible or as easy to reach as men's.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: In the 18th and 19th centuries, women carried numerous possessions on their person and some of these could be worth a lot of money.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: Women were more vulnerable to theft and wealthy women, in particular, worried constantly about pickpockets.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: So – what they did was to have a pair of pockets made that were tied together with string.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: The pockets were made of fabric, which might be recycled cloth if the wearer had little money or something more expensive, such as linen, sometimes featuring very delicate embroidery.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: Women tied the pockets around their waist so that they hung beneath their clothes.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: Remember, skirts were long then and there was plenty of room to hide a whole range of small possessions between the layers of petticoats that were commonly worn.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: They would have an opening in the folds of their skirts through which they could reach whatever they needed, like their perfume.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: Working women, of course, also needed to carry around items that they might use for whatever job or trade they were involved in, but their pairs of pockets still remained on the inside of their clothing, they just got bigger or longer – sometimes reaching down to their knees!

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: So the tie-on pockets went well into the 19th century and only changed when fashion altered towards the end of that period.
segments.zh: 所以，系带口袋一直延续到19世纪，直到那个时期末时尚发生变化才改变。那时裙子变得更紧身、更不臃肿，成对的口袋变得非常显眼——它们太突出，有损女性形象。那些习惯随身携带各种个人物品的女性——并且仍然想这样做——需要有个地方来携带这些物品。那时，小包，或称为“小袋”，开始流行，当然，它们不可避免地导致了更现代的手提包的出现，尤其是当时尚完全取消了口袋的时候。

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: That's when dresses became tighter and less bulky, and the pairs of pockets became very noticeable – they stood out too much and detracted from the woman's image.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: Women who had been used to carrying around a range of personal possessions – and still wanted to – needed somewhere to carry these items about their person.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: That was when small bags, or pouches as they were known, came into fashion and, of course, they inevitably led on to the handbag of more modern times, particularly when fashion removed pockets altogether.

items.prompt: Reason for choice of subject: They are 31 ______ but can be overlooked by consumers and designers.

pairs.note: 题干中的convenient与原文中的convenient完全一致，直接对应。
题干中的overlooked与原文中的overlooked完全一致，直接对应。

paraphrase.explain: 题干问选择这个主题的原因，原文中明确提到'convenient'和'overlooked'，直接定位到这两处即可找到答案。

items.prompt: Pockets in men's clothes: Men started to wear 32 ______ in the 18th century.

pairs.note: 题干和原文都直接提到'pockets'，是关键词复现。
时间状语完全一致，直接定位。
题干说'开始穿'，原文说'变得流行'，意思相同，答案就是'suits'。

paraphrase.explain: 定位句开头提到18世纪，然后说'suits became popular'，与题干'started to wear suits'同义，因此答案填'suits'。

items.prompt: A 33 ______ sewed pockets into the lining of the garments.

pairs.q: sewed pockets into the lining of the garments
pairs.p: pockets were sewn into this cloth
pairs.note: 原文用被动语态'were sewn'对应题干主动语态'sewed'，'this cloth'指代'lining'。
原文'tailor'直接对应题干空白处，表示缝制口袋的人。

paraphrase.explain: 定位句提到'pockets were sewn into this cloth by whichever tailor'，其中'tailor'就是题干所需的名词，且'sewn'与'sewed'同义替换。

items.prompt: Bigger pockets might be made for men who belonged to a certain type of 34 ______.

pairs.note: 'a certain type of' 被替换为 'someone with'，表示具有某种特征的人。

pairs.q: Bigger pockets might be made for men who belonged to a certain type of profession
pairs.p: they were often larger but plainer if the wearer was someone with a profession
pairs.note: 题干描述 'Bigger pockets might be made for men who belonged to a certain type of profession' 对应原文 'they were often larger but plainer if the wearer was someone with a profession'，其中 'larger' 替换 'bigger'，'someone with a profession' 替换 'belonged to a certain type of profession'。

paraphrase.explain: 定位句提到 'they were often larger but plainer if the wearer was someone with a profession'，其中 'larger' 对应 'bigger pockets'，'someone with a profession' 对应 'belonged to a certain type of profession'，因此答案应为 'profession'。

items.prompt: Pockets in women's clothes: Women's pockets were less 35 ______ than men's.

pairs.note: 题干用'less visible'表示'不那么显眼'，原文用'weren't nearly as visible'表达相同意思，都是说口袋不明显。

paraphrase.explain: 题干问女性口袋比男性口袋更不怎么样，原文说女性口袋'weren't nearly as visible'，即不如男性口袋显眼，因此答案就是'visible'。

items.prompt: Pockets were produced in pairs using 36 ______ to link them together.

pairs.note: 题干中的pairs对应原文中的a pair of，都是指一对口袋。
题干中的using表示使用某物连接，原文中的tied together with表示用某物系在一起，意思相同。
题干中的link them together意为将它们连接在一起，原文中的tied together意为系在一起，同义替换。

paraphrase.explain: 定位句提到'have a pair of pockets made that were tied together with string'，其中'string'就是答案，而'pairs'对应'a pair of'，'using'对应'tied together with'，'link them together'对应'tied together'。

items.prompt: Pockets hung from the women's 37 ______ under skirts and petticoats.

pairs.note: 题干空格对应原文'waist'，'their waist'即'women's waist'。
原文'beneath their clothes'替换'under skirts and petticoats'，意思相同。

paraphrase.explain: 定位句提到'Women tied the pockets around their waist so that they hung beneath their clothes'，其中'tied around their waist'对应题干'hung from... waist'，'beneath their clothes'对应'under skirts and petticoats'，因此答案应为waist。

items.prompt: Items such as 38 ______ could be reached through a gap in the material.

pairs.note: such as 和 like 都表示举例，是常见同义替换。

pairs.q: could be reached through a gap in the material
pairs.p: through which they could reach whatever they needed, like their perfume
pairs.note: 题干中的 gap 对应原文中的 opening，reach through a gap 对应 reach through which。

paraphrase.explain: 定位句提到裙子有开口（opening），通过开口可以拿到香水（perfume），与题干中通过材料缝隙拿到物品对应。

items.prompt: When dresses changed shape, hidden pockets had a negative effect on the 39 ______ of women.

pairs.note: detracted from 表示'减损'，与 negative effect on 同义。

paraphrase.explain: 定位句提到隐藏口袋变得明显，减损了女性形象，与题干中'对女性形象有负面影响'对应。

items.prompt: Bags called 'pouches' became popular, before women carried a 40 ______.

pairs.note: 原文直接出现pouches，与题干相同
came into fashion 与 became popular 同义
led on to 表示发展成，对应 before 之后的结果

paraphrase.explain: 定位句提到小包或pouches流行，最终导致手袋出现，因此答案handbag出现在led on to the handbag处。
